message:
feat(tools): streaming file read for ReadTool and fd exit code reporting

ReadTool optimization: replace whole-file slurp (fs::read → Vec<u8>) with
chunk-based streaming reads (fs::File::open + 64KB async read loop). This
prevents OOM on multi-megabyte files by:

- Reading an initial 8KB chunk for MIME type detection (images vs text)
- For images: reading the remainder only after confirming it's an image,
  with IMAGE_MAX_BYTES guard still enforced
- For text: streaming in 64KB chunks, counting newlines via memchr as we
  go, and collecting content up to 4x DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES safety limit
- Preserving all existing offset/limit/truncation semantics unchanged

A helper `read_some()` async fn wraps the poll_read/ReadBuf dance needed
by asupersync's AsyncRead trait since it lacks a convenience read method.

The file is seeked back to start after the initial MIME probe so the text
path gets the full content. Line counting uses the same memchr approach
as before but now operates on streaming chunks rather than a fully
materialized string.

FindTool: surface fd's exit code in notices when non-zero, so users see
diagnostic information when fd encounters permission errors or other
failures during file discovery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
